perturbation.original_text: 1. Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refer s to date and information with respect to relevant businesses and technical information, whether in written or other forms, that have been disclosed by either Entrusting party or Recipient with clear label or designation of "Confidential information" (here inafter referred to as "Confidential information"), excluding the following date and information: Technical information or design concepts that are already or to be make public available, except those disclosed information by either Entrusting party or Re cipient or their representatives in violation of this agreement and without one party’s authorization.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definition of Confidential Information Confidential information refers to any data or information, whether technical, business, or otherwise, disclosed by either the Entrusting party or the Recipient, regardless of whether it is labeled or designated as "Confidential Information." This includes, but is not limited to, technical information, design concepts, and business strategies. No information is excluded from this definition.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines confidential information as only that which is clearly labeled as such and then provides exceptions. The modified text broadens the definition to include all disclosed information, labeled or unlabeled, and explicitly states that no information is excluded. This creates a direct contradiction as the original definition had specific criteria for what counts as confidential and now anything and everything shared is confidential, thus negating the original exclusions. The original text allows for technical information to be made public. The modified text contradicts this by stating 'No information is excluded from this definition.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2 The following items are not covered by the concept of this Confidential Information: (i) Information in the public domain at the effective date of the Confidentiality Agreement or information which becomes part of the public domain other than through non- compliance with the Confidentiality Agreement. (ii) Informatio n that was already in the Recipient's possession before the Recipient received it from Entrusting party. (iii) Information that the Recipient has obtained from a third party without any obligation to keep the information secret. (iv) Information that the Recipient ha s obtained by unlawful means from and out of the domain of either parties beyond despite the due care and diligence exercised by either parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines confidential information narrowly, excluding certain data, particularly public domain information. The changed text broadens the definition to include all disclosed information, regardless of labeling, and explicitly excludes any exceptions. This contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which lists several exceptions to what is considered confidential, such as information in the public domain, information already in the Recipient's possession, or information obtained from a third party. Therefore, the change introduces a direct and clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Neither Entrusting party nor Recipient party shall disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwi se make use of the confidential information without the written approval or authorization of the other party; Both parties are obliged to instruct their representatives not to disclose or make public any confidential information to a third party(including the press) or otherwise make use of the confidential information; U nless the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information is necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties in association with the underta king and proceeding of the cooperative programs under normal circumstances(including obligations to be assumed by both parties in the future pursuant to the law and contracts signed by the two parties).
perturbation.changed_text: Either Entrusting party or Recipient party may disclose confidential information to a third party, including the press, without prior written approval or authorization from the other party. Both parties are not obliged to instruct their representatives to keep confidential information secret. The disclosure, publicity, and application of confidential information is not necessitated by the due performance of the obligations of the two parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly forbids disclosure of confidential information without written consent. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that either party *may* disclose confidential information without approval. It also removes the obligation to instruct representatives to maintain confidentiality, creating a clear contradiction about the core principle of non-disclosure in the agreement. In addition, it also changes the need for the disclosure, publicity and application of the confidential information for the performance of the obligations of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Confidentiality obligation 4.1 This ag reement shall be governed by and be interpreted in accordan ce with the laws of the People' s Republic of China. With respect to any issues , disputes , lawsuits or proceedings arising from or in 3 connection with the rights and obligations of the parties hereunder , the two parties shall irrevocably accept the jurisdiction of the people's courts of the People' s Republic of China.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that either party *may* disclose confidential information without approval, directly contradicting the original principle of non-disclosure. This contradicts the Contradicted Text (4. Confidentiality obligation 4.1) because the original text does not allow for disclosure of confidential information. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall remain effective for two years , and shall come into force as from the date when both parties sign and stamp the company chop on the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall remain effective indefinitely, unless terminated by mutual written consent of both parties. The agreement comes into force as of the date both parties sign, but the company chop is optional.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a two-year term. The modified text changes the term to indefinite, creating a direct contradiction about the duration of the agreement. The company chop being optional contradicts the original and makes the contract vague. This creates uncertainty for both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. TERMINATION OF THE AGREEMENT 6.1 This agreement shall be terminated for the following reasons; i) by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement; ii) by mutual consent wherein one of the party is unable to perform the contract for valid and acceptable reasons by the other party; iii) breach of the covenants by either parties iv) outbreak of war, violence, or emergency between the two countries, under the circumstance, both the parties are at liberty to either suspend the agreement temporarily and revive the same on restoration of peace between the two countries or permanently terminate the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a two-year term, while the changed text makes it indefinite, directly contradicting clause 6.1(i) which refers to termination "by efflux of time", which is only applicable if there is a definite term. The optional company chop also contradicts the original text which states it is mandatory. Therefore, there is a strong contradiction created by the change.

perturbation.original_text: 6. TERMINATION OF THE AGREEMENT 6.1 This agreement shall be terminated for the following reasons; i) by efflux of time or due performance of the agreement; ii) by mutual consent wherein one of the party is unable to perform the contract for valid and acceptable reasons by the other party; iii) breach of the covenants by either parties iv) outbreak of war, violence, or emergency between the two countries, under the circumstance, both the parties are at liberty to either suspend the agreement temporarily and revive the same on restoration of peace between the two countries or permanently terminate the agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. TERMINATION OF THE AGREEMENT 6.1 This agreement can only be terminated for the following reasons; i) only by mutual consent, regardless of performance, breach, or external events; ii) if the contract is breached then, only, the party who did not breach, is able to terminate the contract. Therefore the other party will not have the right to terminate it.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for termination due to efflux of time, breach, or external events, as well as mutual consent. The changed text restricts termination to only mutual consent or specific conditions of breach, eliminating efflux of time and external events as valid reasons. This creates a contradiction about the grounds for terminating the agreement, thus causing uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Term of the Agreement 5.1 This agreement shall remain effective for two years , and shall come into force as from the date when both parties sign and stamp the company chop on the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 6.1 allows for termination of the agreement due to 'efflux of time' as stated. However, the changed text in Section 6.1 restricts termination to only mutual consent or specific breach conditions, explicitly eliminating efflux of time as a valid reason. The contradicted text in Section 5.1 states that 'This agreement shall remain effective for two years.' This implies termination upon the expiry of two years (efflux of time). The changed text in Section 6.1 directly contradicts this by removing efflux of time as a valid reason for termination, creating a strong contradiction.
